content:
VERDICT: ADOPT-A
REASONS: This is the least arbitrary rule because it makes `status` track an evidence/lifecycle event, not whoever happened to create the page first; that directly resolves the flagship-vs-gate inversion without pretending the existing practice was principled. It also preserves page-specific judgment for the mechanical gate pages, which may legitimately deserve `supported` if the status is describing a check that passed rather than an interpretation claim. B merely canonizes the current inconsistency, and C overcorrects by mass-rewriting pages without recording per-page rationale or handling the special case cleanly.  
CONDITIONS: 1) Add the transition rule explicitly to the schema/vocabulary docs, including what counts as a dated event and who can record it. 2) Require a one-line dated normalization note on each touched RESULT page, with the note stating whether `supported` is retained for a passed mechanical check or downgraded to `proposed`.
